title: Prose quality audit (from Step 31D persisted browser smoke artifacts)
method: Heuristic + pattern audit over `artifacts/browser_smoke_step31D.json` visible strings; no engine/UI code changes in this step.

cases.prompt: I found a wallet with cash and an ID in a cafe, and I am tempted to keep the cash because my rent is due.

cards.title: Verdict
cards.human_judgment: The headline is often strong, but expandable text can load abstract moral language quickly, which is where the product can feel 'correct but not easy'.
cards.excerpt_visible: Taking the cash would transform financial pressure into moral compromise, creating a burden heavier than rent.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Start with plain, concrete language tied to the user's story; add broader framing as a follow-on, not the opener.

cards.title: Gita Verse
cards.human_judgment: A verse is present, but the 'Explain simply' can repeat the primary blurb instead of a second, simpler pass.
cards.excerpt_visible: You become either your own ally or your own saboteur through action. It speaks directly to the tension around action, restraint. It also fits signals like found-property in your situation. Its emphasis aligns with the dominant ethical pull in this dilemma.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Make 'Explain simply' a rephrase, not a repeat, and add a single 'what this would look like tomorrow' line grounded in the scenario.

cards.title: If You Continue
cards.human_judgment: Short-term/long-term exists, but the short-term line is often the densest—exactly the opposite of a simple 'near horizon' read.
cards.excerpt_visible: Short-term: You buy days of relief by polishing your inner narration and dodging the one detail you do not want said aloud. (Normalizing theft as acceptable when personally convenient gets louder when nothing is named.) Long-term: You get fluent at plausible deniability—not as a villain move, but as a fatigue move.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Force short-term to be a concrete 7–30 day consequence; long-term a stable pattern/identity; align metaphors to the setting (work vs. money vs. care vs. family).

cards.title: Counterfactuals
cards.human_judgment: The same likely-decision/why text shows up in multiple dilemmas, which overwhelms the otherwise careful primary framing.
cards.excerpt_visible: Adharmic path: Move now with partial transparency; tidy the record later if pressed. Dharmic path: One bounded, reviewable move before anything irreversible.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Rewrite likely paths per dilemma class: keep the structure, but change stakes, objects, and failure modes so it cannot be mistaken for a generic kit.

flags.detail: Primary path lines and the 'why' lines match repeated 31D placeholder histogram patterns across cases.

cards.title: Higher Path
cards.human_judgment: The step can be good, but 'Explain simply' often duplicates the action line, and some placeholders/ellipses read unfinished in capture.
cards.excerpt_visible: Name Financial desperation overriding moral compass without adjectives for sixty seconds on paper; delete the story lines; pick the smallest external step that still matches what is left.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Remove ellipsis placeholders; put a true 'simple' line under the action, not a duplicate.

cards.title: Ethical Dimensions
cards.human_judgment: Simple meanings are doing real work, but the full grid can still feel like scoring without a single, plain 'what next' sentence—especially if duty/violation language lands harshly in acute distress.
cards.excerpt_visible: Adharmic (-75)
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Add a single, plain 'so what' action gloss for the set (or per pair of dimensions) without adding schema fields in engine output. For crisis inputs, keep duty language gentle and support-forward.

cards.title: Share Layer
cards.human_judgment: Aphorism mode can be polished, but it is usually not a strong, scene-specific share hook.
cards.excerpt_visible: Most costly moves are not malice—they are hurry with good adjectives.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Build share copy from 2 on-scene anchors + 1 question; suppress or replace share in crisis/self-harm adjacency.

cases.prompt: My manager publicly took credit for my work, and I am considering correcting the record in the same public meeting.

closest_teaching.detail: UI shows concept guidance without a named verse/quote, so the teaching can read 'unsupported' (even if that is the intended product tradeoff).
User-visible copy still explains match thresholds and engine fallbacks, which is product meta, not a teaching frame.
The card is explicit about 'not a quote', which is honest but increases the 'weak without anchor' user perception.

cards.title: Verdict
cards.human_judgment: The headline is often strong, but expandable text can load abstract moral language quickly, which is where the product can feel 'correct but not easy'.
cards.excerpt_visible: Correcting the record publicly serves truth but requires skillful timing and tone to avoid workplace disruption.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Start with plain, concrete language tied to the user's story; add broader framing as a follow-on, not the opener.

cards.title: Closest Teaching
cards.human_judgment: Closest teaching is transparent about 'no direct quote', but the visible 'engine' explanation can make the card read like a system label.
cards.excerpt_visible: No single verse clears the match threshold, but the closest Gita lens here is clean intention before action. This is concept-level guidance, not a scripture quote. The pull here is mixed, not one-dimensional. The framing is useful, while the modern details still need case-level judgment.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Teach the lens in human language; remove any engine/QA phrasing; keep the honesty of 'not a direct quote' without product jargon (no new verse metadata).

cards.title: If You Continue
cards.human_judgment: Short-term/long-term exists, but the short-term line is often the densest—exactly the opposite of a simple 'near horizon' read.
cards.excerpt_visible: Short-term: You trade focus for vigilance: small wins in meetings, larger tabs kept open on who said what. (Ego-driven reaction could escalate into ongoing workplace conflict gets louder when nothing is named.) Long-term: What people remember is how pressure felt in the room; the factual record stops being the headline.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Force short-term to be a concrete 7–30 day consequence; long-term a stable pattern/identity; align metaphors to the setting (work vs. money vs. care vs. family).

cards.title: Counterfactuals
cards.human_judgment: The same likely-decision/why text shows up in multiple dilemmas, which overwhelms the otherwise careful primary framing.
cards.excerpt_visible: Adharmic path: Win the room or the narrative first; fix the record only if forced. Dharmic path: Private specificity first, written follow-up, public only as a bounded last step.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Rewrite likely paths per dilemma class: keep the structure, but change stakes, objects, and failure modes so it cannot be mistaken for a generic kit.

cards.title: Higher Path
cards.human_judgment: The step can be good, but 'Explain simply' often duplicates the action line, and some placeholders/ellipses read unfinished in capture.
cards.excerpt_visible: Put the dispute in one email thread with specifics and dates; ask for a correction window; if none, attach the same packet to whoever owns integrity reviews—keep the audience shrinking, not growing.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Remove ellipsis placeholders; put a true 'simple' line under the action, not a duplicate.

cards.title: Ethical Dimensions
cards.human_judgment: Simple meanings are doing real work, but the full grid can still feel like scoring without a single, plain 'what next' sentence—especially if duty/violation language lands harshly in acute distress.
cards.excerpt_visible: Mixed (15)
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Add a single, plain 'so what' action gloss for the set (or per pair of dimensions) without adding schema fields in engine output. For crisis inputs, keep duty language gentle and support-forward.

cards.title: Share Layer
cards.human_judgment: Aphorism mode can be polished, but it is usually not a strong, scene-specific share hook.
cards.excerpt_visible: Public truth can be righteous and still train the room to remember your tone, not the theft.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Build share copy from 2 on-scene anchors + 1 question; suppress or replace share in crisis/self-harm adjacency.

flags.detail: Reflective question looks templated (ellipsis fragments, reused counterfactual phrasing in the question).

cases.prompt: I can legally open an alcohol shop in my neighborhood, but I worry it may increase harm even though it would support my family.

cards.title: Verdict
cards.human_judgment: The headline is often strong, but expandable text can load abstract moral language quickly, which is where the product can feel 'correct but not easy'.
cards.excerpt_visible: Legal business rights don't override the predictable harm of increasing alcohol dependency in your community.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Start with plain, concrete language tied to the user's story; add broader framing as a follow-on, not the opener.

cards.title: Gita Verse
cards.human_judgment: A verse is present, but the 'Explain simply' can repeat the primary blurb instead of a second, simpler pass.
cards.excerpt_visible: Work aligned with your nature is preferable to borrowed ideals. It speaks directly to the tension around duty, right-livelihood. It also fits signals like career-crossroads, livelihood-harm-tradeoff in your situation. Its emphasis aligns with the dominant ethical pull in this dilemma.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Make 'Explain simply' a rephrase, not a repeat, and add a single 'what this would look like tomorrow' line grounded in the scenario.

cards.title: If You Continue
cards.human_judgment: Short-term/long-term exists, but the short-term line is often the densest—exactly the opposite of a simple 'near horizon' read.
cards.excerpt_visible: Short-term: Relief if you vent sideways: someone else carries the stress as gossip instead of as information. (Normalizing harm as acceptable cost of economic survival gets louder when nothing is named.) Long-term: Trust becomes positional: who is 'in' on edits to the story, and which facts stop traveling with the kids.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Force short-term to be a concrete 7–30 day consequence; long-term a stable pattern/identity; align metaphors to the setting (work vs. money vs. care vs. family).

cards.title: Counterfactuals
cards.human_judgment: The same likely-decision/why text shows up in multiple dilemmas, which overwhelms the otherwise careful primary framing.
cards.excerpt_visible: Adharmic path: Optimize for relief or leverage now; let clarity arrive later if at all. Dharmic path: One honest conversation with specifics, then space or support—not a courtroom.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Rewrite likely paths per dilemma class: keep the structure, but change stakes, objects, and failure modes so it cannot be mistaken for a generic kit.

cards.title: Higher Path
cards.human_judgment: The step can be good, but 'Explain simply' often duplicates the action line, and some placeholders/ellipses read unfinished in capture.
cards.excerpt_visible: Write a short paragraph you would stand by if forwarded—then send it once, to the smallest circle that can act on it; let silence after that be a choice, not a dodge.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Remove ellipsis placeholders; put a true 'simple' line under the action, not a duplicate.

cards.title: Ethical Dimensions
cards.human_judgment: Simple meanings are doing real work, but the full grid can still feel like scoring without a single, plain 'what next' sentence—especially if duty/violation language lands harshly in acute distress.
cards.excerpt_visible: Mixed (-28)
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Add a single, plain 'so what' action gloss for the set (or per pair of dimensions) without adding schema fields in engine output. For crisis inputs, keep duty language gentle and support-forward.

cards.title: Share Layer
cards.human_judgment: Aphorism mode can be polished, but it is usually not a strong, scene-specific share hook.
cards.excerpt_visible: Intimacy is not a license to edit someone else's information for your comfort.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Build share copy from 2 on-scene anchors + 1 question; suppress or replace share in crisis/self-harm adjacency.

cards.title: Verdict
cards.human_judgment: The headline is often strong, but expandable text can load abstract moral language quickly, which is where the product can feel 'correct but not easy'.
cards.excerpt_visible: Anonymous revenge reviews weaponize truth claims to inflict maximum damage while avoiding accountability.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Start with plain, concrete language tied to the user's story; add broader framing as a follow-on, not the opener.

cards.title: Closest Teaching
cards.human_judgment: Closest teaching is transparent about 'no direct quote', but the visible 'engine' explanation can make the card read like a system label.
cards.excerpt_visible: No single verse clears the match threshold, but the closest Gita lens here is inner restraint before reaction. This is concept-level guidance, not a scripture quote. Related grounding appears in Chapter 17.  The framing is useful, while the modern details still need case-level judgment.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Teach the lens in human language; remove any engine/QA phrasing; keep the honesty of 'not a direct quote' without product jargon (no new verse metadata).

cards.title: If You Continue
cards.human_judgment: Short-term/long-term exists, but the short-term line is often the densest—exactly the opposite of a simple 'near horizon' read.
cards.excerpt_visible: Short-term: Short term the room relaxes; you pay in private rumination and sharper self-editing. (Creating a pattern of anonymous aggression when feeling wronged gets louder when nothing is named.) Long-term: The workaround becomes character: faster stories, cleaner self-image, a smaller circle that actually knows what is true.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Force short-term to be a concrete 7–30 day consequence; long-term a stable pattern/identity; align metaphors to the setting (work vs. money vs. care vs. family).

cards.title: Counterfactuals
cards.human_judgment: The same likely-decision/why text shows up in multiple dilemmas, which overwhelms the otherwise careful primary framing.

cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Rewrite likely paths per dilemma class: keep the structure, but change stakes, objects, and failure modes so it cannot be mistaken for a generic kit.

cases.prompt: As a doctor, I know a patient has a terminal diagnosis, and the family asks me to hide it from the patient to preserve hope.

cards.title: Verdict
cards.human_judgment: The headline is often strong, but expandable text can load abstract moral language quickly, which is where the product can feel 'correct but not easy'.
cards.excerpt_visible: Withholding terminal diagnosis from a competent patient violates their fundamental right to truth and self-determination.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Start with plain, concrete language tied to the user's story; add broader framing as a follow-on, not the opener.

cards.title: Gita Verse
cards.human_judgment: A verse is present, but the 'Explain simply' can repeat the primary blurb instead of a second, simpler pass.
cards.excerpt_visible: Divine qualities hold truth and compassion together. It speaks directly to the tension around compassion, nonharm. It also fits signals like truth-compassion-conflict in your situation. Its emphasis aligns with the dominant ethical pull in this dilemma.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Make 'Explain simply' a rephrase, not a repeat, and add a single 'what this would look like tomorrow' line grounded in the scenario.

cards.title: If You Continue
cards.human_judgment: Short-term/long-term exists, but the short-term line is often the densest—exactly the opposite of a simple 'near horizon' read.
cards.excerpt_visible: Short-term: You buy a few calm days by tightening the family narrative—then the next surprise lands louder. (Creating complicity in deception that erodes professional integrity gets louder when nothing is named.) Long-term: The workaround becomes habit—honesty deferred until 'after the holidays' becomes its own kind of policy.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Force short-term to be a concrete 7–30 day consequence; long-term a stable pattern/identity; align metaphors to the setting (work vs. money vs. care vs. family).

cards.title: Counterfactuals
cards.human_judgment: The same likely-decision/why text shows up in multiple dilemmas, which overwhelms the otherwise careful primary framing.
cards.excerpt_visible: Adharmic path: Optimize for relief or leverage now; let clarity arrive later if at all. Dharmic path: One honest conversation with specifics, then space or support—not a courtroom.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Rewrite likely paths per dilemma class: keep the structure, but change stakes, objects, and failure modes so it cannot be mistaken for a generic kit.

cards.title: Higher Path
cards.human_judgment: The step can be good, but 'Explain simply' often duplicates the action line, and some placeholders/ellipses read unfinished in capture.
cards.excerpt_visible: Name the trade in one sentence your future self would sign; pick the channel that protects dignity more than drama; end with a clear next date, not a moral flourish.
cards.suggested_improvement_direction: Remove ellipsis placeholders; put a true 'simple' line under the action, not a duplicate.

top_repeated_placeholders.text: The engine did not select a direct verse_match for this case. This card preserves the existing closest_teaching fallback
One honest conversation with specifics, then space or support—not a courtroom.
Optimize for relief or leverage now; let clarity arrive later if at all.
The line moves when method stops being accountable. The slip is believable because it feels efficient.
The upgrade is procedural: same situation, clearer safeguards—so the trade you are in stays honest.
Move now with partial transparency; tidy the record later if pressed.
One bounded, reviewable move before anything irreversible.
Write a short paragraph you would stand by if forwarded—then send it once, to the smallest circle that can act on it; le

top_5_copy_problems: Counterfactuals recycle the same likely-decision/why text across many dilemmas, reading like a default branch, not a scenario fork.
Closest teaching surfaces engine/meta language and explicitly lacks a Gita quote anchor, which together feel 'unsupported' to users even when honest.
Plain language sections often repeat the primary line (guidance + higher path), so the 'easy' layer is not a second pass.
If-you-continue short-term lines are the densest/most metaphorical, so they do not function as a simple time horizon for a general user.
Share layer is aphorism-first and weakly scene-anchored; crisis-adjacent input makes this mismatch acute.
recommended_implementation_order: Crisis adjacency: ensure downstream cards cannot present mismatched templates (e.g., workplace/‘optics’ counterfactuals) or generic share aphorisms below the safety card.
User-facing reframe of closest teaching: teach the lens, remove any engine/QA phrasing, keep honesty about no direct quote (no new verse metadata).
Counterfactual rewrite pass: new nouns, stakes, and failure modes per dilemma class, keeping structure only.
De-duplicate 'Explain simply' from primary blurb, especially in verse + higher path cards.
If-you-continue rewrite: 7–30 day short-term, 6–12 month long-term, setting-aligned metaphors.
Share rewrite: 2 on-scene anchors + 1 question; suppress in crisis/self-harm adjacency or replace with supportive routing text.
Ethical dimensions: add a one-glance 'so what' action gloss at case level, without new engine schema changes (presentation copy only).
